pi_items.source: Task description, task.json metadata, setup_task.sh (line 18), export_result.sh (line 18), verifier.py (line 27) - all consistently reference this value as the expected citation type name to be added

Official OpenCAD-php release/stable branch oc_install.sql CREATE TABLE statement; confirmed via GitHub raw URL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/opencad-app/OpenCAD-php/release/stable/sql/oc_install.sql

Official OpenCAD-php release/stable branch oc_install.sql shows citation_types table contains columns: citation_id, citation_description, citation_fine. No column named 'citation_type' exists in official schema. The actual column storing citation type information is 'citation_description'

privileged_info_summary: The task involves adding a new citation type called 'Equipment Safety Violation' to OpenCAD through the admin UI. It operates on the official OpenCAD-php database with a table named 'citation_types'. However, there is a critical schema mismatch: the task metadata claims the column name is 'citation_type', but the official OpenCAD schema defines the column as 'citation_description' (varchar 255). The task setup and verification scripts query for a non-existent 'citation_type' column, which will cause query failures unless the schema is modified or the scripts are incorrect.
